content: Do you ever wonder why it's hot in the summer and cold in the winter? It's because of the way the Earth moves around the sun. The Earth rotates on its side as it orbits the sun, which means that different parts of the planet get different amounts of sunlight throughout the year. When the part of the Earth where you live is tilted towards the sun, you get more sunlight and it's warmer. This is why we have summer. When your part of the Earth is tilted away from the sun, you get less sunlight and it's colder, which is why we have winter. In the spring and autumn, the Earth is tilted just right, so you get a mix of warm and cool weather. This is why we have four different seasons: summer, autumn, winter, and spring. Isn't that cool?
